title: Bash/Shell Scripting
description: Complete shell scripting reference: variables, control flow, functions, text processing, automation, and best practices

commands.name: Script Structure & Shebang
rows:
john@pc:~$ nano hello.sh
#!/bin/bash
echo "Hello World"
john@pc:~$ bash hello.sh
Hello World

commands.name: Variables
rows:
john@pc:~$ nano var.sh
#!/bin/bash
name="John"
echo "Hello $name"
john@pc:~$ bash var.sh
Hello John

john@pc:~$ readonly PI=3.14
john@pc:~$ PI=3.15
bash: PI: readonly variable

commands.name: Special Variables
rows:
john@pc:~$ nano args.sh
#!/bin/bash
echo "Script: $0"
echo "First arg: $1"
echo "All args: $@"
echo "Count: $#"
john@pc:~$ bash args.sh hello world
Script: args.sh
First arg: hello
All args: hello world
Count: 2

john@pc:~$ ls /
bin  home  usr
john@pc:~$ echo "Exit: $?"
0
john@pc:~$ ls /nonexist 2>/dev/null
john@pc:~$ echo "Exit: $?"
2

john@pc:~$ echo "Current PID: $$
12345
john@pc:~$ sleep 5 &
[1] 12346
john@pc:~$ echo "Background PID: $!"
12346

commands.name: Quote Types
rows:
john@pc:~$ name="John"
john@pc:~$ echo "Hello $name"
Hello John

commands.name: String Manipulation
Indexed Arrays
rows:
john@pc:~$ nano for-array.sh
#!/bin/bash
# Define an array
names=("Nabil" "Minhaz" "Fazlul" "Yasin")

# Loop through the array
for name in "${names[@]}"; do
  echo "Hello, $name!"
done
john@pc:~$ bash for-array.sh
Hello, Nabil!
Hello, Minhaz!
Hello, Fazlul!
Hello, Yasin!

john@pc:~$ names=("a" "b" "c")
john@pc:~$ unset names[1]
john@pc:~$ echo ${names[@]}
a c
for f in "${fruits[@]}"; do echo "$f"; done

john@pc:~$ fruits=("Apple" "Banana" "Cherry")
john@pc:~$ for f in "${fruits[@]}"; do echo "$f"; done
Apple
Banana
Cherry

commands.name: Associative Arrays (Bash 4+)
rows:
john@pc:~$ nano user.sh
#!/bin/bash
declare -A user
user[name]="John"
user[age]=25
user[city]="NYC"
echo "Name: ${user[name]}"
echo "Age: ${user[age]}"
echo "City: ${user[city]}"
john@pc:~$ bash user.sh
Name: John
Age: 25
City: NYC

john@pc:~$ declare -A user
user[name]="John" user[age]=25
john@pc:~$ echo ${!user[@]}
name age

commands.name: Arithmetic Operations
rows:
john@pc:~$ nano math.sh
#!/bin/bash
x=5
y=3
echo "Sum: $((x + y))"
echo "Product: $((x * y))"
echo "Division: $((x / y))"
echo "Remainder: $((x % y))"
john@pc:~$ bash math.sh
Sum: 8
Product: 15
Division: 1
Remainder: 2

commands.name: if/elif/else
rows:
john@pc:~$ nano greet.sh
#!/bin/bash
if [ "$1" = "hello" ]; then
  echo "Hi there!"
fi
john@pc:~$ bash greet.sh hello
Hi there!
if [ $age -ge 18 ]; then echo "Adult"; else echo "Minor"; fi

john@pc:~$ nano check-age.sh
#!/bin/bash
age=$1
if [ $age -ge 18 ]; then
  echo "Adult ($age)"
else
  echo "Minor ($age)"
fi
john@pc:~$ bash check-age.sh 20
Adult (20)
john@pc:~$ bash check-age.sh 15
Minor (15)
if [ -f "$1" ]; then echo "File"; elif [ -d "$1" ]; then echo "Dir"; fi

john@pc:~$ nano check-file.sh
#!/bin/bash
path="$1"
if [ -f "$path" ]; then
  echo "File: $path"
elif [ -d "$path" ]; then
  echo "Directory: $path"
else
  echo "Not found: $path"
fi
john@pc:~$ bash check-file.sh /etc/passwd
File: /etc/passwd
john@pc:~$ bash check-file.sh /etc
Directory: /etc

commands.name: for Loop
rows: for name in "Nabil" "Minhaz" "Fazlul"; do echo "Hello $name!"; done

john@pc:~$ nano for-list.sh
#!/bin/bash
for name in "Nabil" "Minhaz" "Fazlul"; do
  echo "Hello $name!"
done
john@pc:~$ bash for-list.sh
Hello Nabil!
Hello Minhaz!
Hello Fazlul!
for i in {1..5}; do echo "Number $i"; done

john@pc:~$ nano for-range.sh
#!/bin/bash
echo "Counting to 5:"
for i in {1..5}; do
  echo "Number $i"
done
john@pc:~$ bash for-range.sh
Counting to 5:
Number 1
Number 2
Number 3
Number 4
Number 5
for (( i=1; i<=5; i++ )); do echo "Count $i"; done

john@pc:~$ nano for-cstyle.sh
#!/bin/bash
for (( i=1; i<=5; i++ )); do
  echo "Count $i"
done
john@pc:~$ bash for-cstyle.sh
Count 1
Count 2
Count 3
Count 4
Count 5
while IFS= read -r line; do echo "$line"; done < file.txt

john@pc:~$ nano read-file.sh
#!/bin/bash
while IFS= read -r line; do
  echo "$line"
done < "$1"
john@pc:~$ bash read-file.sh data.txt
line 1 content
line 2 content

john@pc:~$ nano while-count.sh
#!/bin/bash
count=1
while [ $count -le 3 ]; do
  echo "Count: $count"
  ((count++))
done
john@pc:~$ bash while-count.sh
Count: 1
Count: 2
Count: 3

john@pc:~$ nano until-count.sh
#!/bin/bash
count=1
until [ $count -gt 3 ]; do
  echo "Count: $count"
  ((count++))
done
john@pc:~$ bash until-count.sh
Count: 1
Count: 2
Count: 3

commands.name: Function Basics
rows:
john@pc:~$ nano local.sh
#!/bin/bash
func() {
  local var="local value"
  echo "Inside: $var"
}
func
echo "Outside: $var"
john@pc:~$ bash local.sh
Inside: local value
Outside:

commands.name: read & printf
rows:
john@pc:~$ nano ask.sh
#!/bin/bash
read -p "Enter your name: " name
echo "Hello $name!"
john@pc:~$ bash ask.sh
Enter your name: John
Hello John!

john@pc:~$ cat << EOF
> Hello
> World
> EOF
Hello
World

john@pc:~$ log="info: ok\nerror: fail"
john@pc:~$ grep "error" <<< "$log"
error: fail

commands.name: Error Handling
rows:
john@pc:~$ nano trap-err.sh
#!/bin/bash
trap 'echo "Error on line $LINENO"' ERR
false
echo "This wont run"
john@pc:~$ bash trap-err.sh
Error on line 3

john@pc:~$ nano trap-exit.sh
#!/bin/bash
cleanup() { echo "Cleaning up..."; }
trap cleanup EXIT
echo "Hello"
john@pc:~$ bash trap-exit.sh
Hello
Cleaning up...

john@pc:~$ nano trap-int.sh
#!/bin/bash
trap 'echo "You pressed Ctrl+C"; exit 1' INT
echo "Press Ctrl+C"
sleep 10
john@pc:~$ bash trap-int.sh
^CYou pressed Ctrl+C

commands.name: Debugging
rows:
john@pc:~$ bash -x hello.sh
+ echo Hello World
Hello World

john@pc:~$ bash -n hello.sh
(no output = valid syntax)

commands.name: Redirection
rows:
john@pc:~$ echo "Hello" > file.txt
john@pc:~$ cat file.txt
Hello

john@pc:~$ echo "World" >> file.txt
john@pc:~$ cat file.txt
Hello
World

john@pc:~$ ls /nonexist 2> err.log
john@pc:~$ cat err.log
ls: cannot access '/nonexist': No such file or directory

commands.name: grep / sed / awk / cut
rows:
john@pc:~$ grep -q "error" log.txt && echo "Found error"
Found error

john@pc:~$ echo "old old" | sed 's/old/new/g'
new new

john@pc:~$ sed -n '/START/,/END/p' log.txt
START
content
END

john@pc:~$ echo "a,b,c" | tr ',' '\n'
a
b
c

commands.name: Argument Parsing (getopts)
rows: while getopts "f:o:v" opt; do case $opt in f) file=$OPTARG;; o) output=$OPTARG;; v) verbose=true;; esac; done

john@pc:~$ nano parser.sh
#!/bin/bash
while getopts "f:o:v" opt; do
  case $opt in
    f) file="$OPTARG" ;;
    o) output="$OPTARG" ;;
    v) verbose=true ;;
  esac
done
echo "file: $file"
echo "output: $output"
echo "verbose: ${verbose:-false}"
john@pc:~$ bash parser.sh -f input.txt -o out.txt -v
file: input.txt
output: out.txt
verbose: true

commands.name: Complete Backup Script
